policy_statement: Publish and implement Aotearoa New Zealand's first national adaptation plan (2022–28) as a government-led multi-year work programme prepared under the Climate Change Response Act 2002.
verbatim_text:
This national adaptation plan is published by the Minister of Climate Change under section 5ZT of the Climate Change Response Act 2002.

This plan is a Government-led plan for all New Zealanders that brings together existing actions and proposed future work into a multi-year work programme. Together, these set out what central government will do over the next six years to enable all levels of government, sectors and communities and all New Zealanders to better understand the risks and take action to address them.

## Action 3.5: Support high-quality implementation of climate-related disclosures and explore expansion

The Financial Sector (Climate-related Disclosures and Other Matters) Amendment Act 2021 requires about 200 of Aotearoa New Zealand's largest financial market participants to analyse and publicly disclose their climate-related risks and opportunities each year.

The Government will decide whether to extend mandatory disclosure requirements to public entities by 2024.
